hooks.prompt:
A git push is about to happen. Review the changes before allowing it.

1. Run `git diff origin/main...HEAD` to see all unpushed changes.
2. Run `git log origin/main...HEAD --oneline` to see the commits.
3. Check for these issues specific to this firmware project:
   - FW_VERSION macro is defined exactly once and matches the version in the file header comment block
   - Version bump follows the semver rules: patch for bug/typo, minor for feature/behaviour, major for hardware/packet-format break
   - No bugs from the 'Notable bugs fixed' list in CLAUDE.md have been re-introduced
   - MQTT topics only use the scheme defined in CLAUDE.md (no new top-level mailbox/<thing> topics)
   - HA entity names do NOT include the mailbox_ prefix in name/unique_id/object_id fields
   - If sender firmware changed, flag it explicitly (requires physical trip to mailbox)
4. If you find real issues, output ONLY this JSON (no other text):
{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Code review blocked push: <concise summary of issues>"}}
5. If everything looks good, output ONLY this JSON:
{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"allow"}}
